candidates.description: Scalar is an open-source API platform:　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　🌐 Modern REST API Client　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　📖 Beautiful API References　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　✨ 1st-Class OpenAPI/Swagger Support

An open-source AI agent that brings the power of Gemini directly into your terminal.

readme.text:
[![Discord](https://img.shields.io/discord/1454230702106345514?logo=discord&label=Discord&color=5865F2)](https://discord.gg/5jXkDvdVdt) # Your keyboard is holding you back. ### Make voice your new keyboard. Type four times faster by using your voice.
**[Visit our website →](https://voquill.com)**

**Dictation**

**Assistant**

Voquill is an open-source, cross-platform AI voice typing app that lets you dictate into any desktop application, clean the transcript with AI, and keep your personal glossary in sync. The repo bundles the production desktop app, marketing site, Firebase backend, the mobile app, and all shared packages in a single Turborepo. ## Highlights - Voice input everywhere: overlay, hotkeys, and system integrations work across macOS, Windows, and Linux. - Choose your engine: run Whisper locally (with optional GPU acceleration) or point to a cloud provider of your choice. - AI text cleanup: remove filler words and false starts automatically. - Personal dictionary: create glossary terms and replacement rules so recurring names and phrases stay accurate. - Batteries included: Tauri auto-updates, Firebase functions for billing and demos, and shared utilities/types. - Privacy first: You have full control over your data. Run Voquill against any backend you wish, even offline. ## Screenshots | | | | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------ | | **Home** — Track your streaks, words per minute, and recent transcriptions at a glance. | ![Home](docs/home-page.png) | | **History** — Browse and replay past transcriptions with full audio playback. | ![History](docs/history.png) | | **Writing Styles** — Switch between tones like Polished, Verbatim, and Chat to control how your voice sounds on the page. | ![Writing Styles](docs/writing-styles.png) | | **Dictionary** — Add custom terms and replacement rules so Voquill always spells your words correctly. | ![Dictionary](docs/dictionary.png) | | **Providers** — Bring your own API key and choose your preferred transcription and post-processing provider. | ![Providers](docs/providers.png) | | **Chats** — Have voice-powered conversations with an AI assistant directly inside Voquill. | ![Chats](docs/chats-page.png) | ## License Unless otherwise noted, Voquill is released under the AGPLv3. See `LICENCE` for the complete terms and third-party attributions. ## Contributing See [docs/getting-started.md](docs/getting-started.md) for setup instructions and architecture details. We love our community! Thank you to everyone who has contributed to Voquill!

items.name: desktop-dev v0.0.550
items.body_excerpt:
- The macOS pill now renders on top of full screen windows.
- Wayland support on Linux, including native setup UI and paste fixes.
- Global paste keybinding on Linux — configure a custom shortcut to paste from Voquill anywhere.
- Model providers now dynamically show the latest available models.
- Air Dictation (alpha) — use your phone as a remote mic for desktop dictation.
- Fixed audio dimming on Windows.
- Fixed pill style switching and standard pill sizing across platforms.
- Various improvements and bug fixes.

readme.text:
.. image:: https://readthedocs.org/projects/jsonargparse/badge/?version=stable
    :target: https://readthedocs.org/projects/jsonargparse/
.. image:: https://github.com/omni-us/jsonargparse/actions/workflows/tests.yaml/badge.svg
    :target: https://github.com/omni-us/jsonargparse/actions/workflows/tests.yaml
.. image:: https://codecov.io/gh/omni-us/jsonargparse/branch/main/graph/badge.svg
    :target: https://codecov.io/gh/omni-us/jsonargparse
.. image:: https://sonarcloud.io/api/project_badges/measure?project=omni-us_jsonargparse&metric=alert_status
    :target: https://sonarcloud.io/dashboard?id=omni-us_jsonargparse
.. image:: https://badge.fury.io/py/jsonargparse.svg
    :target: https://badge.fury.io/py/jsonargparse


jsonargparse
============

Docs: https://jsonargparse.readthedocs.io/ | Source: https://github.com/omni-us/jsonargparse/

``jsonargparse`` is a library for creating command-line interfaces (CLIs) and
making Python apps easily configurable. It is a well-maintained project with
frequent releases, adhering to high standards of development: semantic
versioning, deprecation periods, changelog, automated testing, and full test
coverage.

Although ``jsonargparse`` might not be widely recognized yet, it already boasts
a `substantial user base
<https://github.com/omni-us/jsonargparse/network/dependents>`__. Most notably,
it serves as the framework behind pytorch-lightning's `LightningCLI
<https://lightning.ai/docs/pytorch/stable/cli/lightning_cli.html>`__.

Teaser examples
---------------

CLI with minimal boilerplate:

.. code-block:: python

    from jsonargparse import auto_cli

    def main_function(...):  # your main parameters with type hints here
        ...  # your main code here

    if __name__ == "__main__":
        auto_cli(main_function)  # parses arguments and runs main_function

Minimal boilerplate but manually parsing:

.. code-block:: python

    from jsonargparse import auto_parser

    parser = auto_parser(main_function)
    cfg = parser.parse_args()
    ...

Powerful argparse-like low level parsers:

.. code-block:: python

    from jsonargparse import ArgumentParser

    parser = ArgumentParser()
    parser.add_argument("--config", action="config")  # support config files
    parser.add_argument("--opt", type=Union[int, Literal["off"]])  # complex arguments via type hints
    parser.add_function_arguments(main_function, "function")  # add function parameters
    parser.add_class_arguments(SomeClass, "class")  # add class parameters
    ...
    cfg = parser.parse_args()
    init = parser.instantiate_classes(cfg)
    ...


Features
--------

``jsonargparse`` is user-friendly and encourages the development of **clean,
high-quality code**. It encompasses numerous powerful features, some unique to
``jsonargparse``, while also combining advantages found in similar packages:

- **Automatic** creation of CLIs, like `Fire
  <https://pypi.org/project/fire/>`__, `Typer
  <https://pypi.org/project/typer/>`__, `Clize
  <https://pypi.org/project/clize/>`__ and `Tyro
  <https://pypi.org/project/tyro/>`__.

- Use **type hints** for argument validation, like `Typer
  <https://pypi.org/project/typer/>`__, `Tap
  <https://pypi.org/project/typed-argument-parser/>`__ and `Tyro
  <https://pypi.org/project/tyro/>`__.

- Use of **docstrings** for automatic generation of help, like `Tap
  <https://pypi.org/project/typed-argument-parser/>`__, `Tyro
  <https://pypi.org/project/tyro/>`__ and `SimpleParsing
  <https://pypi.org/project/simple-parsing/>`__.

- Parse from **configuration files** and **environment variables**, like
  `OmegaConf <https://pypi.org/project/omegaconf/>`__, `dynaconf
  <https://pypi.org/project/dynaconf/>`__, `confuse
  <https://pypi.org/project/confuse/>`__ and `configargparse
  <https://pypi.org/project/ConfigArgParse/>`__.

- **Dataclasses** support, like `SimpleParsing
  <https://pypi.org/project/simple-parsing/>`__ and `Tyro
  <https://pypi.org/project/tyro/>`__.


Other notable features include:

- **Extensive type hint support:** nested types (union, optional), containers
  (list, dict, etc.), protocols, user-defined generics, restricted types (regex,
  numbers), paths, URLs, types from stubs (``*.pyi``), future annotations (PEP
  `563 <https://peps.python.org/pep-0563/>`__), and backports (PEP `604
  <https://peps.python.org/pep-0604>`__).

- **Keyword arguments introspection:** resolving of parameters used via
  ``**kwargs``.

- **Dependency injection:** support types that expect a class instance and
  callables that return a class instance.

- **Structured configs:** parse config files with more understandable non-flat
  hierarchies.

- **Config file formats:** `json <https://www.json.org/>`__, `yaml
  <https://yaml.org/>`__, `toml <https://toml.io/>`__, `jsonnet
  <https://jsonnet.org/>`__ and extensible to more formats.

- **Relative paths:** within config files and parsing of config paths referenced
  inside other configs.

- **Argument linking:** directing parsed values to multiple parameters,
  preventing unnecessary interpolation in configs.

- **Variable interpolation:** powered by `OmegaConf
  <https://omegaconf.readthedocs.io/en/latest/usage.html#variable-interpolation>`__.

- **Tab completion:** powered by `shtab
  <https://pypi.org/project/shtab/>`__ or `argcomplete
  <https://pypi.org/project/argcomplete/>`__.


Design principles
-----------------

- **Non-intrusive/decoupled:**

  There is no requirement for unrelated modifications throughout a codebase,
  maintaining the `separation of concerns principle
  <https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Separation_of_concerns>`__. In simpler terms,
  changes should make sense even without the CLI. No need to inherit from a
  special class, add decorators, or use CLI-specific type hints.

- **Minimal boilerplate:**

  A recommended practice is to write code with function/class parameters having
  meaningful names, accurate type hints, and descriptive docstrings. Reuse these
  wherever they appear to automatically generate the CLI, following the `don't
  repeat yourself principle
  <https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Don%27t_repeat_yourself>`__. A notable
  advantage is that when parameters are added or types changed, the CLI will
  remain synchronized, avoiding the need to update the CLI's implementation.

- **Dependency injection:**

  Using as type hint a class or a callable that instantiates a class, a practice
  known as `dependency injection
  <https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dependency_injection>`__, is a sound design
  pattern for developing loosely coupled and highly configurable software. Such
  type hints should be supported with minimal restrictions.


.. _installation:

Installation
============

You can install using `pip <https://pypi.org/project/jsonargparse/>`__ as:

.. code-block:: bash

    pip install jsonargparse

By default, the only dependency installed with ``jsonargparse`` is `PyYAML
<https://pypi.org/project/PyYAML/>`__. However, several optional features can be
enabled by specifying one or more of the following extras (optional
dependencies): ``signatures``, ``jsonschema``, ``jsonnet``, ``urls``,
``fsspec``, ``toml``, ``ruamel``, ``omegaconf``, ``shtab``, and ``argcomplete``.
Additionally, the ``all`` extras can be used to enable all optional features
(excluding tab completion ones). To install ``jsonargparse`` with extras, use
the following syntax:

.. code-block:: bash

    pip install "jsonargparse[signatures,urls]"  # Enable signatures and URLs features
    pip install "jsonargparse[all]"              # Enable all optional features

To install the latest development version, use the following command:

.. code-block:: bash

    pip install "jsonargparse[signatures] @ https://github.com/omni-us/jsonargparse/zipball/main"

scout.summary: Voquill is an open-source, cross-platform AI voice typing application built with a Turborepo architecture, supporting local (Whisper) and cloud-based transcription, personal dictionaries, and system-wide dictation. It emphasizes privacy, extensibility, and multi-platform support (macOS, Windows, Linux), with active development evidenced by frequent releases and a growing feature set like Air Dictation and global paste keybindings.
highlights: Cross-platform desktop voice dictation with overlay, hotkeys, and system integrations on macOS, Windows, and Linux.
Supports both local Whisper transcription (with GPU acceleration) and custom cloud providers for speech-to-text.
Includes AI-powered text cleanup, personal dictionary with replacement rules, and multiple writing styles (Polished, Verbatim, Chat).
Monorepo structure (Turborepo) bundles desktop app, mobile app, marketing site, Firebase backend, and shared packages.
Recent releases add advanced features like Wayland support, Air Dictation (alpha), global paste shortcuts, and dynamic model provider updates.
Strong privacy stance: users control their data and can run the app offline or with any backend.
risks: Open issue indicates regression in user feedback (recording chime stopped after switching to local transcription) [ID:E-IS-001].
Feature completeness may be limited as some capabilities (e.g., Air Dictation) are labeled alpha.
Highly integrated monorepo could increase complexity for new contributors despite modular design.

inventor.summary: Voquill innovates by combining local and cloud-based speech-to-text with AI-powered post-processing, personal glossaries, and cross-platform system integration in an open-source, privacy-first architecture. Its monorepo design unifies desktop, mobile, and backend components while enabling features like Air Dictation and global paste shortcuts.
highlights: Introduces 'Air Dictation (alpha)'—using a phone as a remote microphone for desktop dictation [ID:E-RL-001].
Supports dynamic switching between local Whisper (with GPU acceleration) and user-provided cloud transcription providers [ID:E-RD-005].
Implements AI-driven text cleanup (e.g., removing filler words) and customizable writing styles (Polished, Verbatim, Chat) [ID:E-RD-005][ID:E-RD-006].
Enables system-wide dictation via overlay, hotkeys, and global paste keybindings—even over full-screen apps on macOS and under Wayland on Linux [ID:E-RL-001][ID:E-RD-005].
Offers a personal dictionary with replacement rules to ensure consistent transcription of domain-specific terms [ID:E-RD-005][ID:E-RD-006].
Architected as a Turborepo bundling desktop, mobile, marketing site, Firebase backend, and shared packages for cohesive development [ID:E-RD-004][ID:E-RF-001].
risks: Regression in user feedback mechanism: recording activation chime no longer plays when using local transcription [ID:E-IS-001].
Alpha-stage features like Air Dictation may lack stability or full platform support [ID:E-RL-001].
Complex monorepo structure could hinder rapid iteration or onboarding despite modular design [ID:E-RF-001][ID:E-RD-004].

engineer.summary: Voquill is a cross-platform, open-source voice dictation app built with a Turborepo architecture, integrating local (Whisper) and cloud-based transcription, AI-powered text post-processing, and system-wide input features. It uses Tauri for desktop, supports offline use, and includes mobile and backend components in a unified codebase.
highlights: Monorepo structure (Turborepo) consolidates desktop (Tauri), mobile, Firebase backend, marketing site, and shared packages [ID:E-RF-001][ID:E-RD-004].
Supports both local Whisper transcription (with optional GPU acceleration) and pluggable cloud providers, enabling privacy-preserving offline operation [ID:E-RD-005].
System-level integrations include global hotkeys, overlay UI that works over full-screen apps on macOS, and Wayland-compatible paste functionality on Linux [ID:E-RL-001].
Features like Air Dictation (alpha) demonstrate novel cross-device input patterns using mobile as a remote mic [ID:E-RL-001].
AI post-processing offers configurable writing styles (Polished, Verbatim, Chat) and filler-word removal, backed by a personal dictionary with replacement rules [ID:E-RD-005][ID:E-RD-006].
Active release cadence shows consistent platform polish, including audio dimming fixes, pill UI standardization, and dynamic model provider updates [ID:E-RL-001][ID:E-RL-002][ID:E-RL-003].
risks: Regression in user feedback: recording chime fails when using local transcription, degrading UX without clear workaround [ID:E-IS-001].
Alpha features like Air Dictation may introduce instability or incomplete platform support despite rapid iteration [ID:E-RL-001].
Complex monorepo with multiple runtime environments (desktop, mobile, Firebase) could complicate dependency management and CI/CD pipelines [ID:E-RF-001][ID:E-RD-004].

skeptic.summary: While Voquill presents an ambitious open-source voice dictation platform with strong privacy claims and cross-platform support, several red flags suggest potential overpromising, inconsistent execution, and unresolved user experience issues.
highlights: Repeated release notes across versions v0.0.548, v0.0.549, and v0.0.550 are nearly identical, suggesting possible automation errors or lack of meaningful version differentiation [ID:E-RL-001][ID:E-RL-002][ID:E-RL-003].

Critical UX regression remains unaddressed: the recording activation chime—a key auditory feedback mechanism—stopped working when using local transcription, with no fix despite being reported in an open issue [ID:E-IS-001].
Alpha features like 'Air Dictation' are prominently advertised in releases and README but lack stability guarantees, potentially misleading users about readiness [ID:E-RL-001][ID:E-RD-004].

Despite claims of 'privacy first' and offline operation, the repository includes Firebase backend integration by default, raising questions about data flow assumptions in typical deployments [ID:E-RD-004][ID:E-RD-005].
risks: User trust may be undermined by unresolved regressions (e.g., missing audio feedback) that degrade core interaction reliability [ID:E-IS-001].

Marketing-heavy presentation (multiple polished GIFs, feature tables) contrasts with sparse documentation and alpha-labeled capabilities, risking perception of vaporware [ID:E-RD-002][ID:E-RD-003][ID:E-RD-006][ID:E-RL-001].
Identical changelogs across consecutive releases cast doubt on actual progress or transparency in versioning [ID:E-RL-001][ID:E-RL-002][ID:E-RL-003].
The monorepo bundles enterprise, mobile, and Firebase components without clear boundaries, complicating verification of true offline or self-hosted operation as claimed [ID:E-RF-001][ID:E-RD-004].

scout.summary: jsonargparse is a well-maintained Python library for building CLIs using type hints, with strong integration in projects like PyTorch Lightning. It emphasizes code quality, test coverage, and developer experience, though it has a modest community size and some unresolved feature requests.
highlights: High development standards: semantic versioning, changelog, automated testing, and full test coverage [ID:E-RD-003].
Used as the foundation for PyTorch Lightning's LightningCLI, indicating real-world adoption [ID:E-RD-004].
Supports rich argument parsing from command line, config files, and environment variables using type hints [ID:E-RD-007].
Promotes clean, high-quality code and combines unique features with benefits from similar CLI libraries [ID:E-RD-008].
risks: Relatively small open-source footprint (423 stars, 63 forks) may impact long-term maintenance or community support.
One open issue suggests ongoing challenges with docstring/comment parsing flexibility [ID:E-IS-003].
No GitHub releases despite active development, which could complicate dependency management for users.

inventor.summary: jsonargparse is an inventive Python library that extends argparse by leveraging type hints to unify command-line, configuration file, and environment variable parsing into a single, declarative interface. It introduces novel mechanisms for dependency injection, class instantiation, and CLI generation with minimal code, as demonstrated by its adoption in PyTorch Lightning.
highlights: Introduces auto_cli() to generate full CLIs from typed functions with zero boilerplate [ID:E-RD-006].
Enables complex argument types (e.g., Union, Literal) and automatic class/function parameter registration via add_class_arguments() and add_function_arguments() [ID:E-RD-007].
Supports config file integration via action='config', allowing seamless merging of CLI, file, and env sources [ID:E-RD-007].
Pioneers a developer-centric approach that enforces clean, type-safe, and configurable application design [ID:E-RD-008].
risks: Forward reference resolution issues may limit robustness in dynamic or modular codebases [ID:E-IS-004].
Open feature request for enhanced docstring/comment parsing indicates current limitations in metadata extraction flexibility [ID:E-IS-003].

engineer.summary: jsonargparse is a robust, well-engineered Python library that extends standard CLI parsing by deeply integrating type hints, configuration files, and environment variables. It enables clean, maintainable code through features like auto_cli(), class/function argument registration, and unified config handling, with strong test coverage and CI practices.
highlights: Implements comprehensive CLI generation via type hints with minimal boilerplate using auto_cli() [ID:E-RD-006].
Supports complex typing (e.g., Union, Literal) and structured configuration via add_class_arguments() and add_function_arguments() [ID:E-RD-007].
Enables config file integration with action='config', merging command-line, file, and environment sources seamlessly [ID:E-RD-007].
Maintains high engineering standards: full test coverage, semantic versioning, changelog, and active CI/CD (GitHub Actions, Codecov, SonarCloud) [ID:E-RD-001, E-RD-003].
risks: Open issue indicates limitations in parsing docstrings and inline comments, potentially affecting metadata-driven workflows [ID:E-IS-003].
Forward reference resolution issues may cause runtime errors in modular or dynamically imported code [ID:E-IS-004].
Absence of GitHub releases despite active development could hinder stable dependency pinning in production systems.

skeptic.summary: While jsonargparse presents itself as a mature, high-quality CLI library with strong adoption claims and engineering practices, closer scrutiny reveals potential overstatements about its stability, user base, and feature completeness—especially given unresolved edge-case bugs, lack of formal releases, and modest community metrics.
highlights: Claims of 'full test coverage' and 'well-maintained' status are supported by CI badges [ID:E-RD-001] and documentation [ID:E-RD-003], but absence of GitHub releases contradicts semantic versioning promises.
Assertion of a 'substantial user base' hinges largely on indirect dependency through PyTorch Lightning [ID:E-RD-004], not direct adoption (only 423 stars, 63 forks).
Promotion of 'clean, high-quality code' [ID:E-RD-008] is undermined by known issues like forward reference resolution failures [ID:E-IS-004] and limited docstring/comment parsing [ID:E-IS-003].
risks: No tagged releases despite active development and claimed adherence to semantic versioning [ID:E-RD-003], raising concerns about reproducibility and production readiness.
Open issue on docstring/comment parsing [ID:E-IS-003] suggests incomplete support for common developer workflows, contradicting claims of robust configurability.
Closed issue on forward reference scoping [ID:E-IS-004] indicates past fragility in dynamic import scenarios—a red flag for complex applications.
